gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice Flour-Based Foods — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed turnip cake (law bok go) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of ~72 is estimated from the rice flour base, which is the dominant carbohydrate source. Rice flour-based steamed products have published GI values in the range of 65–80 in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The high water content during steaming creates a gelatinised starch matrix that is readily digestible, supporting a high GI estimate. GL per 100g serving = (72 × 17.5) / 100 = 12.6. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 350mg per 100g (2-piece serving) from dried shrimp and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit turnip cake to one order (2 pieces, 350mg sodium) per yum cha session and avoid additional high-sodium dim sum items such as soy sauce dips, har gow, or char siu bao in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~72) from gelatinised rice flour starch; GL ~12.6 per 2-piece serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption to one order (2 pieces, ~100g) and pair with lower-GI dim sum items such as steamed vegetables or tofu-based dishes to moderate the postprandial glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.5g/100g), low cholesterol (8mg/100g), and no trans fat in the steamed version
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The steamed turnip cake is a cardiovascular-friendly dim sum choice due to its very low saturated fat and absence of trans fat; the primary cardiovascular concern is the moderate sodium content (350mg/100g), which should be factored into daily sodium budgets for CVD patients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat; daikon radish provides modest anti-inflammatory glucosinolates and Vitamin C (5mg/100g)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Turnip cake is not a significant concern for rheumatoid arthritis patients; the daikon radish component provides modest anti-inflammatory benefit and the low saturated fat profile is consistent with an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Turnip cake (law bok go / 萝卜糕 / luó bo gāo) is a classic Cantonese dim sum item made by combining finely grated white radish (daikon) with rice flour, dried shrimp, salt, white pepper, and sesame oil into a batter that is steamed in a tray until firm, then sliced and served in pieces. The steamed dim sum version has a soft, slightly gelatinous texture with a mild savoury flavour from the dried shrimp and radish. It is nutritionally distinct from the hawker Chai Tow Kueh version, which is pan-fried or stir-fried in oil with eggs and preserved radish, resulting in approximately double the calorie density (~200+ kcal/100g). The steamed version provides 95 kcal per 100g (2 pieces), 2.5g protein, 2.0g fat, and 17.5g carbohydrate. The primary clinical concerns are: (1) a high glycaemic index (~72) from the gelatinised rice flour starch matrix, producing a glycaemic load of ~12.6 per serving — moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes patients; and (2) moderate sodium (350mg/100g) from dried shrimp and seasoning — moderate concern for hypertension. The daikon radish contributes dietary fibre (1.5g/100g) and Vitamin C (5mg/100g). The food is gluten-free and dairy-free. Culturally, turnip cake is a staple of Cantonese yum cha and is also consumed during Chinese New Year as a symbol of prosperity and rising fortune. It is widely consumed across Singapore, Hong Kong, Malaysia, and the global Cantonese diaspora.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (verified, confidence 1.0 per input record). The food is a well-documented Cantonese dim sum item with consistent nutritional profiles across Singapore food composition sources. GI is an analogous estimate from rice flour-based food literature (confidence 0.9), which is the standard approach for this food type.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for steamed turnip cake is not available in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this specific preparation is not reported in the HPB database entry. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — steamed preparation with no hydrogenated fats). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed zero — daikon radish and rice flour contain negligible retinol or beta-carotene). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin D-containing ingredients). FoodEx2 code A0GJA is used as the closest defensible parent (rice-based dishes); no exact FoodEx2 code exists for law bok go. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on rice flour-based steamed products per University of Sydney GI Database methodology and Atkinson et al. (2008). The input source URL (foodo.sg) is an HPB-affiliated food composition portal and is treated as equivalent to the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for provenance purposes.
